name: Cadre Holdings, Inc.
business: Cadre Holdings is a global leader in manufacturing and distributing safety equipment for law enforcement, first responder, military, and nuclear markets. The company sells body armor, explosive ordnance disposal equipment, duty gear, remote handling solutions, containers for radioactive materials, and ventilation and containment solutions. Through owned distribution, it also serves as a one-stop shop for first responders, offering third-party products like uniforms, optics, boots, firearms, and ammunition.
sellsTo: U.S. federal agencies (DoS, DoW, DoI, DoJ, DHS, DoC, DoE)
namedSuppliers: E.I. du Pont de Nemours Co., Inc.

exposures.sentence: We are a global leader in the manufacturing and distribution of safety equipment and other related products for the law enforcement, first responder, military and nuclear markets.
We are a global leader in the manufacturing and distribution of safety equipment and other related products for the law enforcement, first responder, military and nuclear markets.

The addition of ICOR, acquired in January 2024, gives us the ability to sell a full platform of robots suitable for bomb safety technicians but also meet the needs of law enforcement tactical teams as well.
In 2025, we announced a $50M IDIQ contract with the DoW to provide blast sensors to service members.

catalysts.sentence: In April 2025, the Company acquired Zircaloy for $98.9 million.
In January 2026, the Company acquired TYR Tactical, LLC for $174.0 million.

catalysts.event: IDIQ contract for blast sensors with DoW
catalysts.sentence: In 2025, we announced a $50M IDIQ contract with the DoW to provide blast sensors to service members.

catalysts.event: Launch of Ballast duty holster family
catalysts.sentence: In 2025, we launched a new family of duty holsters, the Ballast.

tamClaims.claim: Serviceable available market for soft body armor (including tactical soft armor) is approximately $870 million.
tamClaims.sentence: Our management estimates the serviceable available market for soft body armor (including tactical soft armor) to be approximately $870 million.

tamClaims.claim: Addressable market for explosive ordnance disposal equipment is approximately $245 million over the seven-to-ten year life cycle of the installed base.
tamClaims.sentence: We also estimate explosive ordnance disposal equipment to have an addressable market of approximately $245 million over the seven-to-ten year life cycle of the products installed base.

tamClaims.claim: Annual addressable market for safety holsters for global law enforcement and military is approximately $380 million.
tamClaims.sentence: Finally, the annual addressable market for safety holsters for the global law enforcement and military is estimated to be approximately $380 million.

tamClaims.claim: Total addressable market for nuclear safety products is between $3 billion and $6 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: Our management estimates the total addressable market for our nuclear safety products to be between $3 billion and $6 billion based on the need for our products by the U.S. government and the U.S. commercial nuclear market.

tamClaims.claim: Addressable number of law enforcement personnel outside the U.S. is approximately 6.1 million.
tamClaims.sentence: Our management estimates our addressable number of total law enforcement personnel outside the U.S. to be approximately 6.1 million, representing a substantial market opportunity.
